pages.raw_text:
The street car companies are
doing business on money which
belongs to their employees. On
this sum, which 1t is said amounts
to more. than $4,000, they do not
pay a cent Of interest or taxes


Every carman in the city is COM
PEELED to deposit $25 with the COM
pany. Perhaps 1t is intended that
this deposit will assist in keeping
the street car men honest.


There are about 1000 of these
employees. A deposit from each one
OF them, accumulated into a fund,
Is cash showing enough for the
companies to do million dollar
business. Fifty thousand dollars is
a whole lot of cash.


AS an Illustration of the meth.
DDS the companies adopt to extort
this deposit from the men Is a
letter which one of them received
yesterday from Superintendent
Farr Of the Inland Empire system.


This employee was one of the few
who has worked for time with
out the Formally of putting up
his $25. Superintendent Farr gave
him just "a hours in which to de-
post the money. The inference
was that unless by the end of that
time the money was up, the en-
ploye would lose his Job. and 1t
would be given to someone who
would put up the deposit.


Street car companies are not the
only ones who hold the public's
money. 1t Is variously estimated
that the gas company holds more
than 100000 in deposits, and the
w. w. p. about the same sum on
light deposits.


The latter companies claim 1t Is
q precaution against anyone de-
frauding them out Of money ow-


,ocdt there seems no possible


M for the street car CON-


is to extort $25 from each car
Nay pay little enough wages.
and $25 will purchase a whole lot
of family necessities. which are
foregone by the heads Of families
on account of the extortion.
